title: Cryptographic Erasure Verification After Decommission

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 3.5.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.17: Cryptographic Erasure Verification After Decommission.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.17' running on index=sec sourcetype="disk:wipe:log"; index=aws eventName DeleteVolume; index=itsm retirement tickets, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Windows (742)
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" earliest=-30d
| where match(short_description,"(?i)decommission|retire") AND match(short_description,"(?i)CDE|PCI|payment")
| rename sys_id as asset_id, number as chg_ticket
| join type=left max=0 asset_id [
    search index=sec sourcetype="disk:wipe:log" earliest=-30d
    | stats max(wipe_status) as wipe_ok by asset_id
  ]
| where isnull(wipe_ok) OR wipe_ok!="VERIFIED"
| table chg_ticket, asset_id, short_description, wipe_ok
description: Correlates disk wipe or crypto-shred completion events with asset retirement tickets so media and VMs that held account data cannot return to production without verified erasure evidence.
value: Correlates disk wipe or crypto-shred completion events with asset retirement tickets so media and VMs that held account data cannot return to production without verified erasure evidence.
implementation: (1) Ensure wipe tooling emits `asset_id` matching CMDB. (2) Define `wipe_status` enumeration with `VERIFIED`. (3) Escalate open rows weekly. (4) Store closure export for PCI media controls.
visualization: Table (exceptions), gauge (% verified), timeline (ticket opened vs wipe event).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: Correlates disk wipe or crypto-shred completion events with asset retirement tickets so media and VMs that held account data cannot return to production without verified erasure evidence — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=sec` `sourcetype="disk:wipe:log"`; `index=aws` `eventName` DeleteVolume; `index=itsm` retirement tickets.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure wipe tooling emits `asset_id` matching CMDB. (2) Define `wipe_status` enumeration with `VERIFIED`. (3) Escalate open rows weekly. (4) Store closure export for PCI media controls.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" earliest=-30d
| where match(short_description,"(?i)decommission|retire") AND match(short_description,"(?i)CDE|PCI|payment")
| rename sys_id as asset_id, number as chg_ticket
| join type=left max=0 asset_id [
    search index=sec sourcetype="disk:wipe:log" earliest=-30d
    | stats max(wipe_status) as wipe_ok by asset_id
  ]
| where isnull(wipe_ok) OR wipe_ok!="VERIFIED"
| table chg_ticket, asset_id, short_description, wipe_ok
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cryptographic Erasure Verification After Decommission (PCI DSS Req 3.2.1, 3.5.1)** — Correlates disk wipe or crypto-shred completion events with asset retirement tickets so media and VMs that held account data cannot return to production without verified erasure evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=sec` `sourcetype="disk:wipe:log"`; `index=aws` `eventName` DeleteVolume; `index=itsm` retirement tickets. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:cmdb_ci. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(short_description,"(?i)decommission|retire") AND match(short_description,"(?i)CDE|PCI|payment")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(wipe_ok) OR wipe_ok!="VERIFIED"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Cryptographic Erasure Verification After Decommission (PCI DSS Req 3.2.1, 3.5.1)**): table chg_ticket, asset_id, short_description, wipe_ok


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (exceptions), gauge (% verified), timeline (ticket opened vs wipe event).
